changes.before: No circle room. No clean floor. No quiet. Only sacks, dust, rough planks, and the buried thread Kel'Thuzad had spent so long teaching dead flesh to answer.
changes.after: Only sacks, dust, rough planks, and the buried thread Kel'Thuzad had spent so long teaching dead flesh to answer.

changes.before: None of it told her as much as the wind when it shifted.
changes.after: The shift in the wind mattered more than the map in her head.

changes.before: The corner of Sylvanas's mouth moved once. "Good. Saves time."
Jaina lost the victory the moment she saw the yard outside.
changes.after: Outside, bread was already in children's hands.

changes.before: Stratholme's people still moved by habit.
changes.after: People still moved through the service lanes on morning errands.

changes.before: He still needed it spoken plain.
The workers nearest the doors did the worst thing possible and froze.
changes.after: The workers nearest the doors froze.

changes.before: Then Arthas would reach the gate and ask the only question that mattered, and she would answer it late.
changes.after: Then Arthas would reach the gate and ask if the city could still be saved, and she would answer too late.
